name: Pacific Crest Bank
description_short: Pacific Crest Bank is a community bank based in Lynnwood, WA offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, and specialized lending products.
description_long:
Pacific Crest Bank is a regional community bank headquartered in Lynnwood, Washington, operating under routing number 125184610. The bank emphasizes relationship-based banking and personalized service rather than one-size-fits-all solutions. The institution has undergone a recent name change and maintains a focus on local business development and community involvement. Their website indicates they serve both personal and business clients with dedicated client services teams.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, and certificate of deposit (CD) products with current rates up to 3.75% APY on 1-year CDs. They provide credit card services for both personal and business use, online banking platforms, and IntraFi Cash Service for account holders. On the lending side, Pacific Crest specializes in portfolio loans, income property lending, construction loans, floating home loans, and adult family home loans—products tailored to the regional Pacific Northwest market.

Pacific Crest distinguishes itself as a community bank by offering relationship-based banking that accounts for client strengths beyond quantifiable metrics. The bank publishes client success stories demonstrating their lending support for local real estate developers and small business owners navigating complex deals. They actively engage in community involvement initiatives and provide accessibility features like AudioEye optimization for their website. The bank also offers practical tools like Remote Deposit Capture (RDC) for businesses and encourages paperless eStatements.

As a single-location community bank with one branch (Alderwood Business Center in Lynnwood), Pacific Crest's reach is geographically limited compared to regional or national banks. While their specialized lending products serve a niche market well, consumers seeking extensive ATM networks, mobile-first banking experiences, or broader product variety may find limitations. The bank appears operationally sound but lacks published information about FDIC insurance limits, specific account fees, or detailed product terms on their website.
services: Business checking accounts with interest options
pros: Specialized lending products tailored to Pacific Northwest market including floating home loans and adult family home loans
Current CD rates competitive at 3.75% APY for 1-year terms and 3.50% APY on 6-month CDs
Dedicated relationship banking approach with personal lending officers and client services teams
Relationship-based pricing advantages for clients with checking or money market accounts
Remote Deposit Capture (RDC) technology available for business clients
Published client success stories demonstrating lending support for local real estate and business development
AudioEye accessibility optimization and electronic disclosure/consent documentation available
cons: Single branch location in Lynnwood, WA limits physical accessibility for customers outside the immediate area
No published information about ATM network size or availability compared to larger regional banks
Limited details on specific account fees, minimum balances, or monthly maintenance costs on website
Website does not clearly outline mobile banking app features or digital-first banking capabilities
No information about overdraft protection, fraud liability limits, or comprehensive account protection details
best_for: Small business owners and real estate developers in the Pacific Northwest seeking relationship-based lending support
Floating home and income property investors requiring specialized mortgage products
Local Lynnwood and greater Puget Sound community members preferring community bank service over large national banks
Businesses needing Remote Deposit Capture and specialized business banking services
diagnosis: Pacific Crest Bank is best suited for small business owners, real estate developers, and property investors in the Pacific Northwest who value relationship banking and need specialized lending products like floating home mortgages. The primary caveat is severe geographic limitation—with only one branch in Lynnwood, WA, this bank serves a hyper-local market and is not practical for customers outside the immediate Puget Sound region or those requiring extensive ATM/branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
